title: Connections, Channels, Heartbeats, Errors, and Recovery · Mastery Check
summary: A production review compares near-miss TypeScript messaging decisions for connections, channels, heartbeats, errors, and recovery and requires the smallest defensible connection, channel, heartbeat, and recovery state machine with changed-case evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for connection, channel, heartbeat, and recovery state machine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use bounded long-lived AMQP connections and explain multiplexed channels, negotiated limits, names, TLS, and lifecycle cost.
Assign channel ownership by publisher or consumer lifecycle and prevent concurrent confirm, acknowledgement, and topology operations from corrupting assumptions.
Choose a heartbeat timeout with network and flow-control evidence and distinguish heartbeat interval, TCP keepalive, and false-positive failure.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Long Lived Connection · changed case · Quiz Long Lived Connection
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz messaging team handles case 3408 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bound heartbeat and reconnect policy while rejecting stale channel-generation callbacks; bound payload, buffer, and retained-ledger bytes; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use bounded long-lived AMQP connections and explain multiplexed channels, negotiated limits, names, TLS, and lifecycle cost.
